prompt: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print, Edo period (1800s), depicting a lone traveler descending a winding mountain path toward a misty valley below, viewed from a high diagonal vantage point in the manner of Hokusai's landscape prints. Flat areas of unmodulated color with no gradients or shading. Bold black outlines of even weight. Muted indigo, ochre, and pale grey palette with cream paper background. Strong diagonal composition guiding the eye from upper right to lower left valley. Visible woodblock grain texture in the sky. Simplified natural forms and atmospheric perspective using layered flat planes. No photographic depth, no cast shadows, no Western perspective techniques. No text, no labels, no modern elements.
tradition_approach: Used Hokusai-style diagonal composition, flat unmodulated color fields, bold even-weight outlines, and a limited indigo-ochre palette with layered atmospheric planes to represent descent toward a minimum.
